_note: Artem verb+preposition collocation lane (RU→EN production). Standing, EXPANDING bank — append new batches (b02, b03…) at each touch, don't rewrite. Origin: 2026-07-05 prep test (~80% but 'very unsure' — confidence deficit on verb-choice/collocation) + 2026-07-08 two-round live session (15/22 on target). Front-loaded with the two isolated residuals from 07-08: agree_to (defaults to 'agree on') and stem_from (avoided both rounds, not in active output). Converting, keep for spaced re-check: prevent/stop-from-gerund, raise/bring-up-with. Run as `translation` in library mode (present prompt_ru, score vs correct_answers, fire common_errors[].regex, else fallback_feedback). Articles stripped by normalize() in scoring — the_overuse is Artem's parallel NOW front, tracked separately, not scored here. See memory project_artem_verb_prep_collocation_lane + coach_notes 07-08.

_coverage._legend: status per collocation: residual=drill-priority gap | recheck=converting, spaced re-check | solid=confirmed 2026-07-08 (log only, no item yet) | seeded=authored in b01 | candidate=b02+ pipeline, not yet authored

TO (accept · direction · attachment).sense: stick to rules (sibling of comply with)

TO (accept · direction · attachment).v: respond to / refer to / revert to

WITH (association · handling).sense: take a matter to sb (the fossil)

ON (reliance · topic).v: depend on / rely on / count on
focus on / act on / bank on

FOR (purpose · cause · provision).v: allow for / provide for / budget for
call for / press for / opt for

IN / INTO (participation · penetration).v: invest in / engage in / specialise in
look into / tap into / delve into

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08 vprep session items 2+6: 'agree TO (accept)' missed twice, defaulted to 'agree on'. with/on legs solid; TO is the gap.
exercises.prompt_ru: Регулятор в итоге согласился на продление сроков.
correct_answers: In the end the regulator agreed to an extension
The regulator eventually agreed to an extension
The regulator finally agreed to extend the deadline
In the end the regulator agreed to extend the deadline

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'agree ON' = two sides settle a point together (a price, a date). Here the regulator is GRANTING/accepting a request → 'agree TO an extension'. Rule: say-yes-to → TO.
✗ 'agree WITH' = share someone's view. Accepting a proposal is 'agree TO'. → 'agreed to an extension'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'agreed TO an extension' (or 'to extend the deadline'). agree to + a proposal/request you accept. Contrast: agree with (a person), agree on (a jointly-decided point).

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08 session: agree_to residual, second exemplar (union refusing to consent).
exercises.prompt_ru: Профсоюз никогда не согласится на сокращение зарплат.
correct_answers: The union will never agree to a pay cut
The trade union will never agree to pay cuts
The union will never agree to a reduction in salaries
The union will never agree to wage cuts

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'agree ON' implies mutual negotiation of the figure. The union is refusing to CONSENT → 'agree TO a pay cut'. say-yes-to → TO.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'agree TO a pay cut'. Accept/consent = agree to. (Bonus: 'a reduction IN salaries', not 'of the salaries'.)

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08 items 3+4: 'stem from' avoided both rounds ('is caused by', 'comes up due to'). Not in active output — force production.
exercises.prompt_ru: Задержка проистекает из нехватки квалифицированного персонала.
correct_answers: The delay stems from a shortage of qualified staff
The delay stems from a lack of qualified personnel
The delay stems from a shortage of skilled staff

common_errors.feedback: ✓ grammatical, but you dodged the target. Force the active collocation: 'stems FROM a shortage of staff'. Tighter exec register than the passive.
✗ 'comes up due to' is loose. Target: 'stems FROM'. Family: result from, arise from — all take FROM.
✗ 'stem' takes FROM only: 'stems FROM a shortage'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'The delay stems FROM a shortage of qualified staff.' stem from = originate in. Block the escapes ('caused by', 'comes up due to').

exercises.prompt_ru: Проблема возникает из-за плохого планирования на ранней стадии.
correct_answers: The problem stems from poor planning at an early stage
The problem stems from poor early-stage planning
The issue stems from poor planning in the early stages

common_errors.feedback: ✗ target is 'stems FROM'. 'comes up' = arises/is mentioned, not the causal-origin sense you want here.
Secondary: 'AT an early stage' / 'IN the early stages', not 'on'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'The problem stems FROM poor planning at an early stage.' stem/result/arise + FROM.

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08: prevent/stop-from-gerund converted 3/3 in round 2 after cold miss round 1. Spaced re-check item.
exercises.prompt_ru: Плохая погода помешала им начать бурение вовремя.
correct_answers: Bad weather prevented them from starting drilling on time
The bad weather stopped them from starting drilling on time
Bad weather prevented them from beginning drilling on time

common_errors.feedback: ✗ prevent/stop + sb + FROM + -ing, never 'to'. → 'prevented them FROM starting'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'prevented them FROM starting drilling'. prevent/stop sb from doing.

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08 item 7: 'raise X with Y' produced clean cold — 4-session fossil, first clean rep. Spaced re-check.
exercises.prompt_ru: Я подниму вопрос бюджета с финансовым комитетом на следующей неделе.
correct_answers: I will raise the budget issue with the finance committee next week
I'll raise the budget with the finance committee next week
I will bring the budget up with the finance committee next week
I'll bring up the budget with the finance committee next week

common_errors.feedback: ✗ THE fossil: you take a matter WITH a person, not TO them. → 'raise the budget issue WITH the committee'. (Note: 'flag' tolerates both to/with; raise/bring up want WITH.)

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'raise the budget issue WITH the finance committee'. raise / bring up X with Y.

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08 extension list: result from (FROM family, reinforces stem from).
exercises.prompt_ru: Убытки стали результатом слабого спроса в этом квартале.
correct_answers: The losses resulted from weak demand this quarter
The losses resulted from soft demand this quarter
The losses stemmed from weak demand this quarter

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'result IN' = leads to (cause→effect). Here demand is the CAUSE, so effect→cause = 'resulted FROM weak demand'. Watch the direction: X results from Y (Y causes X); X results in Y (X causes Y).

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'The losses resulted FROM weak demand.' result from = be caused by. (result in = lead to — opposite.)

exercises.prompt_ru: На Азию приходится сорок процентов нашей выручки.
correct_answers: Asia accounts for forty percent of our revenue
Asia accounts for 40% of our revenue
Asia makes up forty percent of our revenue

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'account FOR' = make up (a share) / explain. → 'accounts FOR 40% of revenue'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'Asia accounts FOR 40% of our revenue.' account for = constitute a proportion (also: explain).

exercises.prompt_ru: В итоге нам пришлось прибегнуть к сокращениям.
correct_answers: In the end we had to resort to layoffs
We eventually had to resort to redundancies
In the end we were forced to resort to layoffs

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'resort TO' (fall back on a measure). → 'resort TO layoffs'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'we had to resort TO layoffs.' resort to = turn to as a last option.

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08 extension list: comply with (compliance register, high-value for Artem).
exercises.prompt_ru: Мы обязаны соблюдать местные нормативные требования.
correct_answers: We must comply with local regulatory requirements
We are required to comply with local regulations
We have to comply with local regulatory requirements

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'comply WITH' (regulations, standards). → 'comply WITH local regulations'. (Note: 'adhere TO' is the sibling that takes TO.)

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'comply WITH local regulations.' comply with = follow (rules). Sibling: adhere TO.

exercises.prompt_ru: Профсоюз возражает против нового графика смен.
correct_answers: The union objects to the new shift schedule
The trade union objects to the new shift pattern
The union objects to the new roster

common_errors.feedback: ✗ Russian 'против' calques to 'against' — but it's 'object TO'. → 'objects TO the new schedule'. (object to + -ing too: 'objects to working weekends'.)

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'The union objects TO the new schedule.' object to + noun/-ing. Not 'against'.

exercises.prompt_ru: Совет директоров настаивает на проведении полного аудита.
correct_answers: The board insists on a full audit
The board of directors insists on conducting a full audit
The board insists on carrying out a full audit

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'insist ON' (+ noun or -ing). → 'insists ON a full audit' / 'insists ON conducting an audit'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'The board insists ON a full audit.' insist on + noun/-ing.

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08 extension list: refrain from (+ gerund; formal register, reinforces FROM family).
exercises.prompt_ru: Просим воздержаться от комментариев для прессы до объявления.
correct_answers: Please refrain from commenting to the press until the announcement
We ask you to refrain from commenting to the press before the announcement
Please refrain from making comments to the press until the announcement

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'refrain FROM' + -ing. → 'refrain FROM commenting'. Same FROM family as prevent/stop/stem/result.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'refrain FROM commenting to the press.' refrain from + -ing.

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08 extension list: contribute to (TO family, direction contrast with result from).
exercises.prompt_ru: Слабый рубль способствовал росту наших затрат.
correct_answers: The weak rouble contributed to rising costs
A weak rouble contributed to our rising costs
The weak rouble contributed to the increase in our costs

common_errors.feedback: ✗ 'contribute TO' (help cause / add to). → 'contributed TO rising costs'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'The weak rouble contributed TO rising costs.' contribute to = help bring about.

exercises.source_observation: 2026-07-08 extension list: benefit from (FROM family, positive-polarity).
exercises.prompt_ru: Проект выиграет от новых инвестиций в инфраструктуру.
correct_answers: The project will benefit from new investment in infrastructure
The project would benefit from new infrastructure investment
The project stands to benefit from new investment in infrastructure

common_errors.feedback: ✗ verb 'benefit FROM' (the noun is 'the benefit OF' — don't cross them). → 'benefit FROM new investment'.

exercises.fallback_feedback: Target: 'The project will benefit FROM new investment.' benefit from (verb). Noun form: 'the benefit OF'.
